mnemonics: CALM (Cayley-Hamilton) Matrix: Every matrix Calms down when Applied to its own Last (characteristic) equation.
keyPoints: A matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation (Cayley-Hamilton Theorem).
Polynomials can be evaluated with matrices by substituting the matrix for the variable.
The eigenvalues of p(A) are p(λ), where λ is an eigenvalue of A.
commonMistakes: Treating matrix multiplication as commutative when evaluating polynomials (e.g., assuming (A)(A²) = (A²)(A) is always true, which it is, but being careless with order for other terms).
Forgetting that constant terms in the polynomial must be multiplied by the identity matrix I.
